paper_title: Synchronization of Greenland and Antarctic Ice Cores

methods.description: The method involves synchronizing Greenland and Antarctic ice cores by identifying large volcanic eruptions with a bipolar acidity or sulfur signature. This is done by matching sequences of eruptions that show the same relative timing in both regions, using annual layer counting to determine the time interval between eruptions.

steps.description: Identify large volcanic eruptions with a bipolar acidity or sulfur or sulfate signature.
steps.searchable_summary: identify large volcanic eruptions with bipolar signatures

steps.description: Match sequences of eruptions that show the same relative timing in both Greenland and Antarctica.
steps.searchable_summary: match eruption sequences by timing in Greenland and Antarctica

steps.description: Perform annual layer counting over the volcanic sequence in both Greenland and Antarctic ice cores.
steps.searchable_summary: perform annual layer counting on ice cores

steps.description: Validate the bipolar link by identifying identical volcanic peak patterns in north and south.
steps.searchable_summary: validate bipolar link by matching volcanic peak patterns

methods.description: This method involves recounting sections of the Greenland NGRIP and Antarctic EPICA Dronning Maud Land ice cores using high-resolution records of chemical impurities, dust, and visual grey-scale intensity to improve precision in dating.

steps.description: Collect high-resolution records of chemical impurities, dust, and visual grey-scale intensity from ice cores.
steps.searchable_summary: collect high-resolution records from ice cores

steps.description: Recount sections of the NGRIP and EDML ice cores using the collected data.
steps.searchable_summary: recount ice core sections using collected data

steps.description: Modify the Greenland timescale based on improved precision from annual counting in both NGRIP and EDML.
steps.searchable_summary: modify Greenland timescale using improved precision

methods.description: This method involves using all available acidity records from various Greenland and Antarctic ice cores to identify volcanic sequences, improving the ability to distinguish large global volcanic events from regional eruptions.

steps.description: Include all available acidity records from Greenland and Antarctic ice cores.
steps.searchable_summary: include acidity records from ice cores

steps.description: Use records of sulfur, sulfate, chloride, and electrical conductivity measurements to identify volcanic signals.
steps.searchable_summary: use chemical records to identify volcanic signals

steps.description: Distinguish large global volcanic events from regional eruptions using the compiled data.
steps.searchable_summary: distinguish global volcanic events from regional eruptions
